item.title: Apple’s WWDC Announcement of the New Apple Intelligence System 摘要
item.description:
Apple announced new Apple Intelligence capabilities powered by next-generation Apple Foundation Models, built in collaboration with Google using Gemini models. These models run on-device and via Private Cloud Compute, emphasizing privacy. The partnership is clarified: Apple uses Gemini models, not the Gemini assistant, ensuring data separation between Apple Intelligence/Siri and the Google Gemini app.
标签: Apple, Google, Gemini, AI, Apple Intelligence, privacy, Private Cloud Compute, Foundation Models, Siri, partnership

item.title: Stop eating Lady Gaga's Oreos 摘要
item.description:
The article contrasts the 1990s anti-commercialism ethos, exemplified by Pearl Jam's refusal to make music videos and avoid 'selling out,' with today's culture where artists like Lady Gaga, Ice Spice, and Bob Dylan openly embrace brand partnerships without stigma. The author argues that the concept of 'selling out' has vanished, replaced by fan excitement for tie-ins. Examples include Megan Thee Stallion's Popeyes franchise, Drake's gambling promotion, and Iggy Pop selling insurance. The piece reflects on how hyper-commercialism, while not new, now faces little backlash, marking a cultural shift from the 90s anti-consumerist sentiment.
标签: selling out, music industry, commercialism, pop culture, 1990s, brand partnerships, anti-consumerism, celebrity endorsements, cultural shift, late capitalism

item.title: Apple Introduces Siri AI 摘要
item.description:
Apple announced Siri AI, a new version of Siri built on Apple Intelligence, enabling personal context understanding across messages, emails, photos, and more. It supports systemwide app actions, onscreen awareness, and web knowledge for answering queries. The author praises the name "Siri AI" as a clear dividing line from old Siri, noting its unique ability to access personal data and perform app actions via App Intents and App Schemas. While less capable than ChatGPT in some areas, it has greater potential due to deep device integration. Success depends on execution, scalability, and third-party adoption.
标签: Apple, Siri AI, Apple Intelligence, AI, personal assistant, App Intents, iOS, generative AI, smartphone, technology

item.title: Apple’s WWDC AI Demos Were Real and in Real Time 摘要
item.description:
Apple's WWDC 2026 AI demos were pre-taped but shot in single takes with no editing, showing real-time interactions like button presses and voice commands. This contrasts with WWDC 2024's slickly produced videos that were more promise than product. The unedited approach emphasizes that if a demo feels slow, the feature itself should be faster, not the video edited.
标签: Apple, WWDC, Apple Intelligence, AI, demos, product development, technology, user experience

item.title: Apple WWDC 2026 Keynote 摘要
item.description:
Apple's WWDC 2026 keynote was notably concise at 76 minutes, including a post-credits Easter egg music video, contrasting with longer keynotes in recent years. The shorter duration suggests a streamlined presentation focusing on key announcements.
标签: Apple, WWDC, keynote, 2026, tech, event, software, iOS, macOS, announcement

item.title: The Talk Show Live From WWDC: Tonight, In-Person and Streaming 摘要
item.description:
The Talk Show Live from WWDC 2026 is happening tonight at the California Theater. Tickets are still available for in-person attendance. The show can also be watched live in stereoscopic immersive format via the Theater app from Sandwich Vision on Vision Pro, priced at $12.99, which includes replay access with bonus features.
标签: WWDC, live show, The Talk Show, California Theater, Vision Pro, immersive streaming, Sandwich Vision, Theater app, tickets, tech event

item.title: Quoting Andrej Karpathy 摘要
item.description:
Andrej Karpathy reflects on how AI-generated software, exemplified by Claude Fable 5, is transforming software development. He notes that as working software becomes easily accessible, demand for it grows substantially (Jevons paradox). Users can now request custom explainers, visualizers, dashboards, bespoke single-use apps, expanded test suites, auto-optimized code, and large research projects with tailored HTML results. This shift encourages a mindset of limitless possibilities, freeing users to imagine and create without traditional constraints.
标签: AI, LLMs, software development, Jevons paradox, generative AI, Anthropic, Andrej Karpathy, productivity, custom software, innovation

item.title: Apple OS 27: The Small Things 摘要
item.description:
Rishi Ó highlights that the most meaningful Apple updates are not flashy features but subtle improvements that fix annoyances, smooth workflows, and refine rough edges. These 'small things' demonstrate Apple's commitment to craftsmanship. He compiled a list from WWDC26 screenshots of improvements coming later this year, presented as bullet points for easier reading.
标签: Apple, WWDC, iOS, macOS, user experience, software updates, design, quality of life, technology

item.title: Setting a custom price for a model in AgentsView 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison explains how to set a custom price for a model in AgentsView, a tool by Wes McKinney for tracking token usage and costs across coding agents. When Claude Fable 5 was released but not yet in AgentsView's pricing database, he reverse-engineered the tool to add custom pricing. He shares a screenshot showing his cost breakdown by project, with prod_datasette_agent costing $74.06 (89.3% of total). The tool also displays cache efficiency, noting $516.62 saved vs. uncached usage.
标签: AI, LLM, pricing, AgentsView, token usage, cost tracking, coding agents, reverse engineering, Claude Fable 5, cache efficiency

item.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026 摘要
item.description:
Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday set a record with nearly 200 security fixes, including three dozen critical vulnerabilities and three publicly exploited zero-days. The surge is attributed to increased AI-assisted bug discovery. Notable flaws include CVE-2026-49160 (IIS denial of service reported by OpenAI), CVE-2026-45586 (Windows Collaborative Translation Framework privilege escalation), and CVE-2026-50507 (BitLocker elevation of privilege). A researcher named Nightmare Eclipse, claiming to be a former Microsoft employee, released exploits and plans more. Microsoft also patched a Visual Studio Code zero-day allowing GitHub token theft. Additionally, 360 browser vulnerabilities were fixed, and Microsoft's repositories were hit by the Shai-Hulud worm.
标签: Microsoft, Patch Tuesday, security, zero-day, vulnerability, Windows, AI, supply chain attack, Chrome, BitLocker
